User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the Van-Vert application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.

role.description: User's role, determining their access level.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user was created.

id.description: Unique identifier for the application entity.

licenseType.description: The type of license being applied for.

submittedAt.description: Timestamp when the application was submitted.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the application was last updated.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the application was created.

feedback.description: Internal notes from admins, visible to the user.

flightLogs.description: A list of flight logs submitted by the applicant.

flightLogPdfUrl.description: Public URL of the uploaded flight log PDF in Firebase Storage.

documents.description: An array of documents associated with the application.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Represents a document uploaded for an application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the document entity.

fileType.description: Type of the uploaded file (e.g., PDF, JPG, PNG).

fileSize.description: Size of the uploaded file in bytes.

fileUrl.description: Public URL of the uploaded file in Firebase Storage.

expiryDate.description: Expiry date of the document, if applicable.

status.description: Document status (e.g., missing, uploaded, needs attention).

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp when the document was uploaded.

isExpiringSoon.description: Flag indicating if the document is expiring soon.

requiresExpiry.description: Whether this document requires an expiry date.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification for a user.

userId.description: The ID of the user who this notification is for.

body.description: The main content of the notification.

href.description: A URL to navigate to when the notification is clicked.

isRead.description: Whether the user has read the notification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was created.

definition.description: Stores user profiles and roles. This data is critical for the Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system. Head Admins can manage roles, while Admins have limited update capabilities.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Top-level collection for all applications. Security rules ensure users can only access their own applications. Reviewers can view all applications and modify document statuses, while Admins can change the overall application status.

params.name: applicationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the application.

definition.description: Subcollection storing notifications for a specific user. Notifications are created by Admins when an application's status changes.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure supports a granular, four-tier role-based access control system (User, Reviewer, Admin, Head Admin). Placing applications in a top-level collection allows privileged roles (Reviewer and above) to query across all submissions, a critical feature for the review process. Security is enforced through Firestore rules that check user roles and document ownership, ensuring a secure and scalable architecture.

*   **Users (`/users/{userId}`):** The source of truth for user roles, essential for RBAC.
*   **Applications (`/applications/{applicationId}`):** A flat collection enabling cross-user queries for admins/reviewers, while restricting regular users to their own data via security rules.
* **Notifications (`/users/{userId}/notifications/{notificationId}`):** A secure, user-specific subcollection for private notifications.
